case_name: Deanna McKeehan v. Delaware Neurosurgical Group

summary.brief: Deanna McKeehan v. Delaware Neurosurgical Group. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: In August 2019, an ambulance transported McKeehan, who had been involved in a motor vehicle collision, to Christiana Care Hospital.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The scan revealed two unruptured brain aneurysms—one in her middle cerebral artery and the other in her internal carotid artery bifurcation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Boulos recommended that McKeehan undergo an aneurysm clipping procedure, which involved placing small ligation clips across the entry point of each aneurysm to block blood flow.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In September 2019, Dr. Boulos performed the procedure.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: During the exam, Dr. Boulos discovered that McKeehan’s body was impaired—she could not bend her left arm or left leg.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Several hours after the surgery, a physician’s assistant ordered a CT scan for McKeehan, and the results evidenced signs of a stroke.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: McKeehan’s neurosurgical expert, Dr. John Diaz-Day, testified on cross-examination that a stroke is a 'known complication' of the surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. John Diaz-Day

evidence_index.text: Defense neurological expert, Dr. Owen B. Samuels, testified that a stroke is a 'routine' complication of the surgery due to the 'nature of neurosurgery and aneurysms and neurosurgical-related issues.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Owen B. Samuels

evidence_index.text: The judgment of the Superior Court is AFFIRMED.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.text: The jury was instructed on Elements of Medical Negligence, Burden of Proof by a Preponderance of the Evidence, Definition of Medical Negligence, and Proximate Cause.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Involved in a motor vehicle collision
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient was transported by ambulance to the hospital following a motor vehicle collision. A CT scan revealed two unruptured brain aneurysms.

focused_exam.neurological: Impairment noted post-surgery; patient could not bend left arm or leg

requestables.name: CT Scan
requestables.description: CT scan of the brain to assess for aneurysms

reveal.clinical_significance: Requires surgical intervention to prevent rupture
reveal.decision_impact: Led to recommendation for aneurysm clipping procedure

requestables.name: Post-operative CT Scan
requestables.description: CT scan ordered post-surgery to assess for complications

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to hospital following a motor vehicle collision

key_events.event: Post-operative CT scan ordered showing stroke

decision_points.prompt: How should the surgeon respond to reduced blood flow to the brain?

options.description: Adjust the ligation clip to restore blood flow
options.clinical_reasoning: Restoring blood flow is critical to prevent ischemic complications

options.description: Do not adjust the ligation clip
options.clinical_reasoning: Believed stroke was a known complication and did not act

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not adjust the ligation clip
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed stroke was a known complication

expected_action_court.description: Adjust the ligation clip to restore blood flow
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was necessary to prevent stroke

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to adjust the clip led to reduced blood flow and stroke
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Clip should have been adjusted to restore blood flow
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Impairment in left arm and leg movement

key_findings: Stroke was a known complication of the procedure
No deviation from standard of care found

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Adjusting the clip could have prevented the stroke

factual_timeline.event: Patient transported to hospital and CT scan performed

court_findings.finding: No deviation from standard of care
